info.title: Transfers API
info.description:
# General
Bookaway B2B API give you access to all Bookaway inventory. You can use it to search, book and get the status of a specific booking.
# Authentication
To authenticate to Bookaway B2B API you need first to get credentials a `client_id` and a `client_secret`.
Bookaway B2B API uses Oauth2 Client Credentials to perform the authentication. The Bookaway Oauth2 flow documentation is available [here](https://app.swaggerhub.com/apis-docs/Bookaway/Oauth/1.0.0).
# Environments
### Sandbox
The sandbox environment is used for test and development purposes. It works the same as the production environment but return slightly different data and requires to use sandbox credentials.
  The base URL for each API call is:
  
  `https://stage.bookaway.com/api/b2b/v1`
  
### Production
Once you’re finished developing your integration with Bookaway B2B API in the sandbox environment, and after reviewing your integration with Bookaway, you’ll be given production credentials.
  The base URL for each API call in production is:
  
`https://www.bookaway.com/api/b2b/v1`


# Workflow
### 1. Static API
You need first to retrieve the different destinations and transfers information that Bookaway offers. The destinations are available as JSON file. We recommand run an update on a weekly basis.

  They’re composed of 5 types of entities:

  * **Stations**: Pickup/Dropoff point with specific coordinate
  * **Cities**: List of available destinations
  * **Routes**: 
    
    * A route is a way to go from a station A to a station B. Example: Bangkok to Pattaya
  
    * A route is defined by a direction, Cusco to Machu Picchu is a route and Machu Picchu to Cusco is another route.
    
    * On a specific route there can be different types of transfers: train, bus, ferry, private car, etc…
  * **Transfers**: A transfer is a bus/train/ferry operated on a specific route. Each transfer has a operator
  * **Operators**: List of available operators.
  
Static API returns thousands of results, you should never send live requests but instead download `stations.json`, `cities.json`, `routes.json` , `transfers.json` and `operators.json` files on a daily basis and cache them in your system.

### 2. Trips API

  Let you search through all trips available in Bookaway B2B API.
  
  You can also look at a specific Trip.
  
  Trips might require additional information, some information is static which can be retreived from the transfers static endpoint.
  Some information is dynamic like seatMap - trips which might require seatMap input will have a flag on them `hasExtraUserInputRequired` as `true`.
  For those trips a call to `tripDetails` should be made. If an aditional info returned, its should be used on the booking process (selected seats for example).
  
  
  
### 3. Bookings API

  Let you book a specific Trip.
  
  Let you see the status of a specific booking. 
  
  Let you cancel a specific booking.
  
### 4. Post Bookings WebHooks

  Booking updates can be retrieved by getting booking status - from Booking API, or by a webhook notification which will notify about booking updated.
  
  All webhooks will be delivered to the same endpoint which should response with a HTTP 200 status.
  
  The webhooks can be differentiated by `type` field
  
  All webhooks will use HTTP **POST**

  **Please note:**
  
  The paths stated below are only used to make the documentation clearer to read and navigate. Nothing should follow the '/' in the path.
  
  for example `https://webhook-endpoint/approved` will be sent to your webhook url without the `/approved` safix
    contact:
name: omer chehmer
    contact:
email: omer@bookaway.com

parameters.name: limit

get.description:
Return a json file containing all the available transfers with all their static information - images, description, amenities, ect.

Please note that some transfers also has extras and additional passenger information  which  require to provide in the booking request.
Search available trips between stations for a specific date. Supports search by city to city as well.

parameters.name: authorization
parameters.description: an authorization header as "Bearer xxxxxxxxxxxxxx"

parameters.name: departureStation
parameters.description: list of departure station ids, seperete by comma (,)

parameters.name: arrivalStation
parameters.description: list of arrival station ids, seperete by comma (,)

parameters.name: fromCity
parameters.description: Search for trips departing from a specific city

parameters.name: toCity
parameters.description: Search for trips arriving to a specific city

parameters.name: departure
parameters.description: the required date in format: "YYYY-MM-DD"

parameters.name: passengers
parameters.description: amount of passengers [1..15], if not supplied, calculated attributes will assume single passenger

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: limit the number of results. default is 100, maximum allowed is 5000

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: result offset. default is zero.

parameters.name: authorization
parameters.description: an authorization header as "Bearer xxxxxxxxxxxxxx"

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id of the Trip

parameters.name: passengers
parameters.description: amount of passengers [1..15], if not supplied, calculated attributes will assume single passenger

parameters.description: an authorization header as "Bearer xxxxxxxxxxxxxx"

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id of the Trip

message.example: The trip does ont have extra dynamic details.

post.description: Book a specific Trip, booking consist of selected trip, and passengers info. Note that some trips requires extra information per passenger, this extra info is described in the transfers API.
Call this API after creating a booking. A booking can be approved only after this API is called.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Id of the booking as was returned by the create booking API

message.example: The booking with id "{id}" does not exist.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Id of the Booking

message.example: The booking with id "{id}" does not exist.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Id of the booking, please verify cancellation policy for this transfer before.

message.example: The booking with id "{id}" does not exist.

post.description: Webhook to notify a booking has been approved
Webhook to notify a booking has been cancelled
Webhook to notify a booking has been updated

from.description: Unique id that identifies the departure station of the route

to.description: Unique id that identifies the departure station of the route

name.description: Name of the route in english

id.description: Unique id that identifies the station
Unique id that identifies the operator

isPrintedVoucherRequire.description: When true, a printed voucher is needed, otherwise, e-ticket is accepted

id.description: Unique id that identifies the transfer

departureStation.description: Unique id that identifies the departure station of the transfer

arrivalStation.description: Unique id that identifies the arrival station of the transfer

description.description: Marketing description in English of the Transfer

isAmendable.description: Is a booking made on this transfer amendable

amendments.description: List of amendments fees by time before departure. Will be populated if `isAmendable` is `true`

timeBefore.description: Number of time units prior to departure that a booking is amendable

fee.description: The fee that will be charged when amending a booking at this time

isRefundable.description: `true` if refund is offered in case of booking cancellation

cancellations.description: List of cancellation fees by time before departure. Will be populated if `isRefundable` is `true`

timeBefore.description: Number of time units prior to departure that a booking is cancellable

fee.description: The fee that will be charged when cancelling a booking at this time

isPrivate.description: Private transport is a vehicle that serves the passenger only and is limited in seat number.

isLateApproval.description: Booking will be approved more than 24h after payment

maxPassengers.description: Valid only for private transport, maximum number of seats a the vehicle.

passengerAlert.example: Don't forget to add your passport number, nationality, age and gender. Bookings without this information will NOT be approved.

voucherRemark.description: Supplier's remark to the passenger on the voucher
voucherRemark.example: Please be at the departure point 30 minutes before departure time to show your passport or valid ID during check in.

TripDetails.description: Return additional trip details regarding a specific trip, all fields are optional, might return empty object

reason.description: In case of `failed` status, describes the failure reason

voucherPdf.description: link to a pdf voucher once the booking is approved

noteFromSupplier.description: extra info provided by the supplier once approved

items.description: array of items which represent trips that were booked

bookingCode.description: opeartor booking code which sometimes is required by the operator upon boarding

items.description: an array of the seats for the ride

VehicleClass.description: The class level of the vehicle

id.description: Unique identifier of the extra option

extraType.description: The type of the extra option

priceLevel.description: the price level of the extra (if display_once or display_once_muiltiply_per_direction the extra is applied for the booking itself (and for booth items when multileg booking), if display_A_to_B it should be the item)

upgrade.description: If `extraType`=`upgrade` then the extra is described here. (Example "Buffet Lunch")

mandatory.description: If true, then this extra option is mandatory

needInput.description: If true, then a user input is needed (example for user input "Hilton hotel, main plaza")

perPassenger.description: If true, the price is for a single passenger and will be multipy by number of passengers.

note.description: Additional instructions regarding this extra option
note.example: Pickup is limited to city center area

PassengerExtraInfo.description: Value may be a string, or an enum if data attribute is defined

id.description: Unique identifier of the passenger extra info

label.description: The name of the extra info to provide

type.description: The type of the passenger extra info value

data.description: A list of possible values (eg. Gender, Nationality, etc... )

extraInfos.description: If the transfer requires passenger extra info, if should be supplied for each pasenger

definition.description: Unique identifier of the passenger extra info, taken from the transfers api

value.description: The value of the passenger extra info, type is string/enum as described on transfers api

definition.description: The id of an extra option to book

value.description: Some extra options needs input from the passenger

items.description: each item in the items array describes a trip to be booked.

tripId.description: The id of the trip to book

extraOptions.description: List of requested extra options suggested in the Transfer, with price level of display_once or display_once_muiltiply_per_direction

seats.description: List of selected seats (this is only required on trip which has additional details with seatMap)

extraOptions.description: (optional) List of requested extra options suggested in the Transfer, with price level of display_once or display_once_muiltiply_per_direction
